inputPayload.situation: the enterprise pricing change, where it is unclear who owns the call because pricing sits between sales and finance and there is no single owner

captures.renderedOutputText:
Authority over the enterprise pricing change, where it is unclear who owns the call because pricing sits between sales and finance and there is no single owner — unclear authority: nobody accountable will sign a price floor while both functions claim a veto is diffused: no single accountable owner holds the right to decide, so unclear authority: nobody accountable will sign a price floor while both functions claim a veto persists as a vacuum every function can defer into. A secondary pricing_pressure signal is present and may become primary if the case evolves.
An unowned decision is made by default — by the calendar, by the loudest function, or by a competitor — and accountability arrives only at post-mortem. In this case the stated exposure is Q3 enterprise renewal revenue repricing by default at renewal.
Within the next 10 minutes, start the first step: Write the decision as one sentence and circulate it to the commercial director and each implicated function within 24 hours. Then continue: Name the single owner in writing, including the explicit right to decide against either function's preference.
If a single accountable owner were named tomorrow, would the decision still be stuck? If yes, ownership is not the binding constraint and this diagnosis is wrong. Test it against the enterprise pricing change, where it is unclear who owns the call because pricing sits between sales and finance and there is no single owner — unclear authority: nobody accountable will sign a price floor while both functions claim a veto before acting on this judgement.
Escalate when no owner can be named within one working week, or when two functions both claim — or both refuse — the decision in writing.
Write the decision as one sentence and circulate it to the commercial director and each implicated function within 24 hours.
Name the single owner in writing, including the explicit right to decide against either function's preference.
Have the owner set the decision date and the minimum evidence they need by the Q3 renewal cycle opening in five weeks.
Record the ownership assignment where the next dispute will find it.
This judgement classifies the case as ownership_ambiguity from 13 matched input signals; signals absent from the input could not be weighed. The common false move for this pattern — commissioning more analysis, which feels like progress but lets the ownership vacuum persist. — was assumed avoidable; if it is already underway, the sequence above needs re-sequencing.

inputPayload.caseTitle: When Growth Models Broke Under Tariff Shock
inputPayload.signal: Tariff repricing moved faster than consensus allocation models.
inputPayload.decisionLesson: Reclassify the regime before consensus catches up; waiting for confirmation is itself the exposure.
inputPayload.consequence: Late movers absorbed avoidable drawdown and reallocated under worse liquidity.
inputPayload.nextMove: Run a regime-break stress test against all growth-dependent allocations and set a 72-hour tariff-event decision protocol.
inputPayload.limitation: Public dossier proof does not expose private source records or regulated financial advice.
inputPayload.reuseValue: Reusable as a board-level test for structural-break decision latency.

captures.renderedOutputText:
Tariff repricing moved faster than consensus allocation models.
Late movers absorbed avoidable drawdown and reallocated under worse liquidity.
Run a regime-break stress test against all growth-dependent allocations and set a 72-hour tariff-event decision protocol.

Reusable as a board-level test for structural-break decision latency.

inputPayload.caseTitle: The Illusion of Team Alignment Under Pressure
inputPayload.signal: Leadership alignment confidence diverged materially from team respondent evidence.
inputPayload.decisionLesson: Do not restructure on leadership self-assessment; measure execution-layer alignment first.
inputPayload.consequence: Proceeding would have amplified divergence and increased dissonance within 90 days.
inputPayload.nextMove: Halt restructuring pending execution-layer alignment measurement for affected units.
inputPayload.limitation: Public dossier proof does not identify private respondents or internal records.

inputPayload.caseTitle: Why Escalation Was Denied (And That Saved the System)
inputPayload.signal: Escalation pressure was high while evidence readiness was low.
inputPayload.decisionLesson: Separate urgency from evidence sufficiency; premature escalation can multiply the failure.
inputPayload.consequence: Premature board escalation would have targeted symptoms and burned escalation credibility.
inputPayload.nextMove: Complete evidence gathering across all active failure domains before board engagement.
inputPayload.limitation: Public dossier proof does not expose source-level records or confidential stakeholder details.
inputPayload.reuseValue: Reusable as an escalation-readiness threshold before high-stakes governance intervention.

inputPayload.decisionDescription: allocating the 20% budget reduction, where leadership can't decide between the three cut options and has been deliberating for six weeks with another review scheduled

captures.renderedOutputText:
allocating the 20% budget reduction, where leadership can't decide between the three cut options and has been deliberating for six weeks with another review scheduled is frozen, not pending: the option set has been stable while deciding keeps restarting, and every deferral has been justified by asking for another review that produced no new information means the daily default — the status quo — is itself the most expensive option on the table. The instrument records 2 evidence items and 1 irreversible element(s): the group imposes a uniform cut that everyone agrees is the worst outcome.
Paralysis is a decision for the status quo made daily without anyone owning it — it pays the worst option's cost without its accountability. In this case the stated exposure is irreversible exposure: the group imposes a uniform cut that everyone agrees is the worst outcome.
Shrink and timebox: eliminate options to two, set a decision date inside ten working days, and pre-agree what happens by default if the date passes undecided. Record the decision and its basis by the revised budget is due to the group in two weeks; reopening it requires new information, not new discomfort. Re-open this instrument when that step completes, when the pressure level changes, or within fourteen days — whichever arrives first.
